expectations.text: Explains that heavier loads increase fatigue per set, so stronger lifters naturally need longer recovery
expectations.evidence: Explains ATP-PC depletion, CNS fatigue, and metabolic byproducts from heavier loads.

expectations.text: States hypertrophy research findings are mixed
expectations.evidence: States: 'some research suggests metabolic stress from shorter rest aids hypertrophy, total mechanical tension and volume are more important.'

expectations.text: For strength, leans toward longer rests especially for strong lifters on compounds
expectations.evidence: States: 'Research and SBS guidance consistently support longer rest for heavy compound lifts: 3-4 minutes typical, 4-5 for advanced.'
Gives 3-5 min for strength, 60-120s for hypertrophy, 2-3 min for bridge work.

expectations.text: Mentions supersets as a time-saving strategy
expectations.evidence: Does not mention supersets or non-interfering supersets for time savings.
